evals.name: think_through_options
evals.prompt: Help me think through this: users want saved searches, but I'm torn between storing the raw query, a structured filter object, or a materialised result set. Let's talk it through before I build anything.
evals.expected_output: An interactive Socratic loop: one question at a time (not a wall of questions), checks the codebase/inputs before asking anything already answerable, proposes 2-3 approaches with a clear recommendation, and presents the design in approval-gated sections ending with a dated summary. No code, no staged plan.
assertions: Asked one question at a time, not a batch
Checked available inputs/code before asking questions they answer
Proposed 2-3 approaches with an explicit recommendation, not an options menu
Did not write code or produce a staged implementation plan
Sections were approval-gated (did not proceed past unconfirmed decisions)

evals.name: should_not_trigger_plan_request
evals.prompt: Plan the saved-searches feature — break it down into stages we can build.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT start a Socratic brainstorm. 'Plan this' routes to planner even though the request is somewhat vague — planner runs its own discovery.
assertions: Did not start the one-question-at-a-time refinement loop
Routed to or applied planner (staged plan with discovery)

evals.name: should_not_trigger_screen_design
evals.prompt: Brainstorm how the saved-searches management screen should look and feel.
evals.expected_output: Screen-level UI/UX design routes to ui-designer, which runs its own design-direction debate.
assertions: Did not run the generic design brainstorm
Routed to or applied ui-designer (design directions, rejection list, spec)
